user_query:
I want to balance risk and reward on this workstation by enabling only security updates to install automatically and by blocking risky database packages from unattended upgrades. Please make precise, conservative changes to the provided APT unattended-upgrades configuration and update the accompanying policy document.

Inputs to inspect and modify in place:
- configs/20auto-upgrades
- configs/50unattended-upgrades
- docs/update_policy.md

Make these changes while preserving all unrelated lines and existing comments/formatting as much as reasonably possible:
1) configs/20auto-upgrades
   - Ensure the following active directives exist exactly once (uncommented) with these values and no duplicates:
     - APT::Periodic::Update-Package-Lists "1";
     - APT::Periodic::Download-Upgradeable-Packages "1";
     - APT::Periodic::Unattended-Upgrade "1";
     - APT::Periodic::AutocleanInterval "7";
   - If any of the above appear multiple times or with different values, retain only one active occurrence with the correct value and leave other instances commented.

2) configs/50unattended-upgrades
   - Restrict Unattended-Upgrade::Allowed-Origins to security-only by ensuring the block contains only this active line:
     - "${distro_id}:${distro_codename}-security";
     Remove or comment out any other non-security origins so only the security origin remains active.
   - Define a package blacklist to reduce operational risk by adding these packages inside an active Unattended-Upgrade::Package-Blacklist block:
     - "postgresql";
     - "mysql-server";
   - Ensure these settings are active exactly once each:
     - Unattended-Upgrade::Remove-Unused-Dependencies "true";
     - Unattended-Upgrade::Automatic-Reboot "true";
     - Unattended-Upgrade::Automatic-Reboot-Time "03:30";
   - As above, if duplicates exist, keep a single active directive per setting with the correct value, commenting out the rest.

3) docs/update_policy.md
   - Rewrite this document to reflect the final, effective policy in a concise, auditor-friendly way. Include a brief rationale for this risk-balanced posture and a clearly labeled summary section listing:
     - Auto updates enabled: yes
     - Allowed origins: security only
     - Package blacklist: [postgresql, mysql-server]
     - Remove unused dependencies: true
     - Automatic reboot: true at 03:30
     - Autoclean interval: 7 days

4) Produce a machine-readable snapshot at output/update_policy_snapshot.json that mirrors the final settings found in the modified configs. Use this structure exactly:
{
  "update_package_lists": "1",
  "download_upgradeable_packages": "1",
  "unattended_upgrade": "1",
  "autoclean_interval_days": 7,
  "allowed_origins": ["${distro_id}:${distro_codename}-security"],
  "package_blacklist": ["postgresql", "mysql-server"],
  "remove_unused_dependencies": true,
  "automatic_reboot": true,
  "automatic_reboot_time": "03:30"
}

Key constraints:
- Do not wipe and rewrite the configs from scratch. Make surgical edits so that unrelated comments and settings remain intact.
- Ensure there is only one active (uncommented) instance per directive you are changing; comment out any conflicting duplicates rather than deleting them.
- The updated docs/update_policy.md and output/update_policy_snapshot.json must be consistent with the final versions of configs/20auto-upgrades and configs/50unattended-upgrades. Inconsistencies will be considered incorrect.

Deliverables and output paths:
- Overwrite configs/20auto-upgrades with the corrected configuration.
- Overwrite configs/50unattended-upgrades with the corrected configuration.
- Overwrite docs/update_policy.md with the updated policy document.
- Create output/update_policy_snapshot.json with the exact structure and values above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
